methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Mimic Robotics is an ETH Zurich spinout at the Seed stage that has raised $20M+ to date, including a $16M seed round. The Robotics & Manufacturing sector comprises 9 private peers and 1 public comparable (277810.KQ), though the narrow peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage matches for reliable median-based valuation.

Stage-progression analysis, the primary applicable method, uses the $20M total raised as a proxy base and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage transitions, implying a $50M valuation. A peer-median approach was not executable: while 11 same-sector robotics companies exist in the dataset, filtering for stage alignment and valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints, making cross-sectional comparison unreliable. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material bias.

The triangulated valuation range of $42M to $57M (median $50M) reflects stage-progression mechanics alone. This analysis carries material uncertainty given the single-method foundation and the absence of tight peer comparables at seed stage in manufacturing robotics. Key dependencies include (1) the validity of the 2.5x multiplier for this subsector and stage, (2) whether the $20M raised figure approximates fair-market capital value, and (3) the trajectory of downstream fundraising and burn. Investors should weight this assessment heavily toward illustrative rather than definitive, pending access to deeper cohort data or company-specific operational metrics.
